Metals Comparative Signal
← Back to dashboard

Metals Comparative Signal

Regime-aware, multi-factor cross-metal signal with rolling relative value, z-scores, momentum, and macro USD adjustment.

As of 2026-04-03

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement (mandatory): Given an Aluminium Z-score of 3.415 and widespread Tactical Trim signals across Gold and Silver, this setup aligns with Downward-biased price paths and Elevated volatility, where the dominant risk is Mean reversion, not Trend continuation. (1)
  • The signal is currently in a "Moderate Outflow Bias" regime, triggered by high-volatility dislocation and extreme overvaluation in cross-metal ratios. (1)
  • Conviction Band: High; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No. Signal Stability Assessment: Deteriorating; Threshold Proximity: Near; Revision Sensitivity: Low.

Methodology Applied:

  • Regime-adaptive weighting (high_vol_dislocation) prioritizes Relative Value (40%) over Standardisation (30%) and Momentum (30%) to address price extremes. (1)
  • Standardisation rules trigger "Tactical Trim" when Z-scores exceed 1.5–2.0 and "Exhaustion Risk" above 3.0 (e.g., Aluminium). (1)
  • Macro adjustment factor (0.85) scales scores based on USD strength, which currently acts as a drag on the composite signal. (1)
  • Metals Comparative Signal; latest observation: 2026-04-03. (1)

Key Dynamics:

  • Extreme Relative Value Layer (-0.8) is driven by Silver's 95th+ percentile overvaluation against Gold, Platinum, and Copper. (1)
  • Positive Standardisation Z-scores across all major metals (except Zinc) indicate price levels are significantly above historical means. (1)
  • Strong USD acts as a macro-adjustment headwind, further depressing the Composite Score to -0.299. (1)
  • Conditional Invalidation: A sudden reversal in USD strength or a drop in Aluminium/Silver Z-scores below 1.5.
  • Stability: The signal shows persistent overextension, with Copper and Gold moving deeper into "Trim" territory over the last three days. (1)

Scenario Balance:

  • Base Case dominant: Tactical mean reversion as metals pull back from overextended Z-scores.
  • Upside risk secondary: Continued momentum squeeze if macro USD strength unexpectedly collapses.
  • Downside risk residual: Sharp liquidation if "high_vol_dislocation" triggers broader cross-asset selling.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Tactical (weeks) as signals focus on "Trim" and "Exhaustion" thresholds.
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: High due to strong conviction (0.75) and cross-component alignment.

Macro Relevance:

  • Macro dimension: Liquidity and Pricing Extremes.
  • Economic mechanism: Direction implies an "Outflow Bias," where high valuations and USD strength pressure margins and speculative positioning. (1)
  • Cycle position: Not determined.
  • Typically interacts with inflation expectations and real yield signals to confirm if metal prices are "too hot."

Regime Context:

  • Newly entered "high_vol_dislocation" regime as of late March, following a period of rapid price appreciation. (1)
  • Direction of change: Strengthening outflow bias as Z-scores remain elevated despite high USD strength. (1)

Model Limitations:

  • Relies on ratio-based mean reversion which can be delayed during structural commodity super-cycles.
  • USD adjustment factor is a broad proxy and may not capture specific metal-to-dollar sensitivities.

Data & References:

  • Metals Comparative Signal (2026-04-03). (1)
  • Influential points: Aluminium Z-Score (3.415) and Silver_vs_Gold percentile (95.3). (1)
  • Public datasets: LME Warehouse Stocks and COMEX Positioning data would improve reliability.

Metals Comparative Dashboard v1.2 (Regime-Adaptive)

Rolling relative value, regime-aware z-scores, momentum and composite layers, plus silver regime score and z-score percentile context.

Methodology
The information on this website is provided for general informational and educational purposes only and does not constitute financial, investment, legal, or tax advice. It does not take into account any individual objectives, financial situation, or needs.

All views expressed are personal, based on publicly available information, and do not represent the views of any employer or reflect any proprietary or internal analysis. This information should not be relied upon for making investment decisions.

No representation or warranty is made as to the accuracy, completeness, or timeliness of the information, and no liability is accepted for any loss arising directly or indirectly from its use.
